unit.note: Full shared/tests/ suite — 2007 passed, 11 skipped, 18 deselected, 1 pre-existing failure (see degraded.preexisting_canon_lint_failure). New tests added in this iterate: 20 markdown_table.escape_cell unit tests (8 boundary categories + table-row round-trip + None/non-string coercion) + 3 build-dashboard regression tests (the AC-4 cell-count assertion driven against the real renderer). All compliance plugin tests still green (320/320). touches_shared_infra risk flag enforced the full run.

e2e.note: No web/Playwright surface — markdown-rendering bug fix. CLI surface verified below.

surface_verification.note: F0.5 cli surface — 23 tests (20 unit + 3 integration regression). The integration tests drive the real generate_dashboard renderer with a synthetic shipwright_events.jsonl, parse the rendered markdown row with re.split(r'(?<!\\)\|', row), and assert exactly 6 data cells. Exit 0, single attempt.

degraded.detail: External LLM review provider keys (OPENROUTER_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY) not configured in this environment per check-external-review-keys.py. Per the iterate skill's degraded-mode rule (Branch B Option 2), fell back to the mandatory self-review + an internal code-reviewer subagent pass — the latter caught a missed wrap site in compliance_report.py::_external_review_evidence past the mini-plan's 5-file modify list and was fixed in the same iterate. Marker at .shipwright/planning/iterate/external_review_state.json.

shared/tests/test_artifact_path_canon.py::test_no_legacy_artifact_paths[compliance-migrated] fails on shared/scripts/tools/record_event.py:374 where 'compliance' appears as a quoted argparse choice in choices=['docs','tooling','compliance','infra']. Introduced by PR #42 (fd212b2 chore(phase-0a): backfill FRs + add change_type schema field). Verified by stash-and-rerun on the base commit — fails identically on origin/main with this iterate's changes stashed. Out of scope for this iterate (markdown table escaping); a separate single-line iterate should add the inline marker '# artifact-path-canon: legacy' to that line, mirroring the precedent at shared/scripts/lib/drift_parsers.py:110 which suppresses the same word for the same reason.
